architecture.summary: ZARA is a training-free agentic framework that reasons over motion time-series by retrieving evidence from a statistically grounded textual knowledge base and iteratively selecting discriminative cues to perform grounded reasoning over candidate activities. The system distills reference sensor data into natural-language priors, which are retrieved to guide LLM-based reasoning without parameter updates to the language model.

biblio.title: ZARA: Training-Free Motion Time-Series Reasoning via Evidence-Grounded LLM Agents

claims.statement: ZARA generalizes robustly to unseen subjects and across datasets, demonstrating strong transferability across heterogeneous sensor domains
Applying LLMs directly to numerical time-series often leads to hallucinations and weak grounding
ZARA distills reference data into a statistically grounded textual knowledge base that transforms implicit signal patterns into verifiable natural-language priors
ZARA operates in a training-free inference setting, requiring no parameter retraining to adapt to new behaviors
